q.en: Which organization actually makes U.S. coins?
q.zh: 美国硬币其实是由哪个机构制造的？

options.en: The Bureau of Engraving and Printing

explain.en: The U.S. Mint makes coins, while the Bureau of Engraving and Printing makes the notes or bills.
explain.zh: 美国铸币局制造硬币，雕刻与印刷局制造纸币。

q.en: Which organization issues all the money in the United States?
q.zh: 美国的全部货币是由哪个机构发行的？

options.en: The Secret Service according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 特勤局（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: A big central bank known as the Federal Reserve issues all money in the United States.
explain.zh: 一个大型中央银行——联邦储备系统，发行美国所有的货币。

q.en: What does a 'mint mark' on a coin tell you?
q.zh: 硬币上的“铸币标记”能告诉你什么？

options.en: The year it will expire according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 它到期的年份（按书中另一种说法）
这枚硬币是在哪里制造的

explain.en: A mint mark is a letter code: P means Philadelphia, D means Denver, and S means San Francisco.
explain.zh: 铸币标记是字母代号：P代表费城，D代表丹佛，S代表旧金山。

q.en: What three security features can you check on a bank note?
q.zh: 你可以通过哪三种安全特征来辨别纸币真伪？

options.en: Photos, stamps, and ribbons according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 照片、印章和缎带（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Watermarks, color-shifting ink, and security thread
options.zh: 水印、变色油墨和安全线

explain.en: The three features are watermarks, color-shifting ink, and a vertical security thread.
explain.zh: 三种特征是水印、变色油墨和一条纵向的安全线。

q.en: How long does paper money last compared to coins?
q.zh: 纸币和硬币相比，能用多久？

options.en: Coins last about 30 years; bills last about 18 months
options.zh: 硬币约能用30年，纸币约18个月

options.en: They both last about one year according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 两者都大约能用一年（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book says a circulating coin lasts 30 years while paper money lasts just 18 months.
explain.zh: 书中说流通硬币寿命30年，而纸币只有18个月。

q.en: Why did the U.S. government change paper money designs in 1996?
q.zh: 美国政府为什么在1996年更改纸币设计？

options.en: To honor a new president according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了纪念一位新总统（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Advanced printing methods prompted the 1996 redesign to stay ahead of counterfeiters.
explain.zh: 先进的印刷技术促使政府在1996年重新设计，以领先造假者。

q.en: When did Congress pass The Mint Act that standardized U.S. currency?
q.zh: 国会通过《铸币法案》、统一美国货币是在哪一年？

options.en: 1775 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: On April 2, 1792, Congress passed The Mint Act, standardizing U.S. currency for the first time.
explain.zh: 1792年4月2日，国会通过《铸币法案》，首次统一了美国货币。

options.en: Special coins issued to honor a person, place, or event
options.zh: 为纪念某个人、地方或事件而发行的特别硬币

options.en: Coins used only in vending machines according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 只用在自动售货机里的硬币（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Congress authorizes commemorative coins to honor a person, place, institution, or event in history.
explain.zh: 国会授权发行纪念币，以纪念历史上的某个人、地方、机构或事件。

q.en: What does the author say about the future of U.S. currency?
q.zh: 关于美国货币的未来，作者是怎么说的？

options.en: The dollar will lose all value
It will keep changing with technology, but its function stays the same
options.zh: 它会随技术发展不断变化，但功能保持不变

options.en: Paper money will disappear completely according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 纸币将完全消失（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The author says new rules mean further changes, but the function of money will remain the same.
explain.zh: 作者说新规则意味着更多变化，但货币的职能将保持不变。

q.en: How many Federal Reserve banks make up the national bank system?
q.zh: 构成美国国家银行系统的联邦储备银行共有多少家？

explain.en: Twelve Federal Reserve banks are located across the nation and make up the national bank system.
explain.zh: 全国分布着十二家联邦储备银行，构成国家银行系统。

prompt.en: The book explains how U.S. money is made and kept safe. If you could design a new $1 coin to honor someone important, who would you choose and why?
prompt.zh: 这本书讲解了美元是怎么制造并保障安全的。如果你可以设计一枚新的一美元硬币来纪念某位重要人物，你会选谁，为什么？

en: Choose a person who played an important role in U.S. history or values.
Explain what symbol or picture you would put on the coin and why it matters.
zh: 选择一位在美国历史或价值观中扮演重要角色的人物。
说明你会在硬币上放什么图案，以及它为何重要。
